title: Metals gain as supply issues mount
section_path: Metals gain as supply issues mount
section_path_text: Metals gain as supply issues mount
summary:
Main
Most sectors were lower on hopes that negotiations between Iran and the US would lead to a peace deal. By Daniel Hynes
Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-05-20 164151.png | assets/2026-05-20_metals-gain-as-supply-issues-mount_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_8db38b736372.png

Market Commentary
Crude oil prices edged lower as traders weighed up the spectre of the US resuming military strikes on Iran against a possible peace deal. President Trump warned that if Iran didn't agree to US peace terms, "we may have to give them another big hit".

children.title: Chart of the Day
children.summary: Global lithium prices continue to push higher on constructive demand trends, underpinning momentum in China's spot market. Tight inventories and rising feed-stock costs are also lifting chemical prices.
